mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal contemporaneous aggregate supply expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations and cause positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a two-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption declines and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in greater than 50% of triggered walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) alongside an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4%) generate a negative equity risk premium shock as markets price recession risk under restrictive policy, producing negative 20-day returns.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with slowing growth
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint signal instances over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, sharp month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) transmit through the residential investment multiplier into broader demand contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60d returns are non-negative in greater than 50% of tightening-cycle housing decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) rising more than 0.15% month-over-month signals accelerating wage income and consumption, reducing near-term recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered walk-forward weeks.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) running above a 3% annualized three-month rate forces upward repricing of real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Average 20d forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and decreases in unemployment (UNRATE) confirm a virtuous demand-employment loop that reduces macroeconomic volatility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 0.5% annualized signals aggregate output expansion that revises earnings expectations upward before they are fully discounted, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP surprises fail to coincide with positive 20d returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.
